rationale: Rebuilt the branch binary at the fix commit 2b55d1a7 and re-ran all 8 round-1 fixtures unchanged: identical results, zero regression (path-like zero/multi-owner still fail impacted-domains-resolve/SevError; bare-name-no-manifest still passes; both anchor forms of a missing ADR still fail adr-touchpoint-missing/SevError; existing anchors, bare-prose mentions, and the 110-shaped spec still pass; no adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant leakage). Added the two round-2 boundary cases plus one extra polarity: (C9) [ADR-12345] -> no diagnostic; (C10) [ADR-00311] with ADR-0031 existing -> no diagnostic; (C11, my own addition) [ADR-00311] with ADR-0031 ABSENT -> still no diagnostic. C11 is the case that actually isolates the bug: under round-1's regex this input would have truncated to ADR-0031 and, since ADR-0031 doesn't exist in that fixture, would have wrongly emitted adr-touchpoint-missing for an ID the author never wrote. To confirm this precisely I isolated both regex literals (pre-fix vs 2b55d1a7) in a standalone Go snippet outside the repo and ran them directly against the four boundary strings: the old regex truncates both '[ADR-00311]'->'ADR-0031' and '[ADR-12345]'->'ADR-1234' (confirming the exact defect G1 found), while the new regex produces NO match for either and still matches genuine 4-digit bare-ID and filename-form anchors identically to before. The fix is structural, not coincidental, and introduces no new error class. Worktree left clean.
findings: All 8 round-1 fixtures re-run against 2b55d1a7 produce byte-identical results to round 1 -- no regression from the digit-boundary change.

Isolated regex comparison (old `\[(ADR-\d{4})[^\]]*\]\([^)]+\)` vs new `\[(ADR-\d{4})([^0-9\]][^\]]*)?\]\([^)]+\)`) run standalone against '[ADR-00311](...)' and '[ADR-12345](...)': old regex truncates to ADR-0031/ADR-1234 respectively (the exact bug); new regex matches neither at all.

New polarity C11 ([ADR-00311] with ADR-0031 absent) confirms the fix is a true structural non-match, not a coincidence that only holds when the truncated-to ID happens to exist -- the round-1 regex would have produced a false adr-touchpoint-missing here for an ID nobody wrote; the fix now produces no diagnostic regardless of ADR-0031's existence.

Genuine 4-digit bare-ID and filename-form anchors (existing and missing) are extracted identically to round 1 -- the digit-boundary group is purely additive at the malformed-ID edge, no behavior change at the valid-ID core.
